wine_bars.name: Enoteca Picone
wine_bars.wine_program: Historic enoteca since 1946 with 7,000-plus labels spanning Sicilian artisan producers alongside global grands crus; retail shop by day, wine bar from 5pm.
wine_bars.description: Palermo's most storied enoteca since 1946; floor-to-ceiling bottles, an encyclopedic Sicilian list and an expert team that stocks wines rarely found elsewhere on the island.
wine_bars.tip: Arrive at 5pm for aperitivo with a charcuterie board before the evening rush.

wine_bars.name: Enoteca Butticè
wine_bars.address: Piazza San Francesco di Paola 12, 90138 Palermo (PA), Sicily
wine_bars.wine_program: 700-plus labels led by Sicilian selections curated by sommelier Salvatore Fanale; aperitivo platters and outdoor terrace.
wine_bars.description: Beloved Palermo wine bar where sommelier Salvatore Fanale fields a vast list of Sicilian gems and global stalwarts; outdoor terrace on the piazza is the place to be at sundown.
wine_bars.tip: Ask Salvatore for his current Etna picks; the underground gems change seasonally.

verified.address_quoted: piazza San Francesco di Paola, Palermo

wine_bars.name: Bottiglieria del Massimo Champagneria
wine_bars.wine_program: Family-run champagne specialist since 1968; also carries Sicilian still wines, French sparkling and an extensive spirits collection.
wine_bars.description: Long-running bar near Teatro Massimo, open from 9:30am to 1am, with outdoor terrace; champagne and sparkling focus alongside well-priced Sicilian wines.
wine_bars.tip: Great aperitivo stop before an opera at Teatro Massimo; try a glass of Sicilian bubbles with the house tramezzini.

verified.address_quoted: Via Salvatore Spinuzza, 59, 90133 Palermo PA, Italy

wine_bars.name: Enoteca Allegra
wine_bars.wine_program: 320-plus labels covering all Italian regions; sommelier Andrea Allegra curates a cellar focused on Italian elegance with 70 off-list rarities; modern wine dispensers for by-the-glass service.
wine_bars.description: Sleek Lungomare wine bar opened by sommelier Andrea Allegra after experience abroad; 320 Italian labels with 70 off-list rarities, priced from EUR 25 a bottle, in a refined seaside setting.
wine_bars.tip: The off-list section includes micro-producer Etna wines not found in retail; ask Andrea to unlock it.

wine_bars.name: Nelson Sicily
wine_bars.wine_program: Specialist Sicilian shop and wine bar with nearly all major island producers represented by the glass and bottle; second location in the San Benedetto monastery at Via Crociferi 14.
wine_bars.description: Started as an online enoteca in 2013; now two storefronts on the same Baroque street with a wine-bar format and one of Catania's fullest Sicilian selections by the glass.
wine_bars.tip: The monastic setting at Via Crociferi 14 is the more atmospheric option for a glass.

wine_bars.name: Enoteca Solaria
wine_bars.wine_program: 2,000-plus labels dominated by small-production natural and artisan wines from Italy and worldwide; dual-purpose retail and wine bar with food pairings.
wine_bars.description: Ortigia institution since 1987, transformed into a leading natural-wine bar by AIS-certified owners Elisa Merlo and Gianfilippo Russo; triple Slow Wine award winner for best Cerasuolo di Vittoria selection.
wine_bars.tip: Slow Wine has cited Solaria for three consecutive years for Sicily's best territorial selection.

wine_bars.name: Franco Wine Bar
wine_bars.wine_program: Sicilian cellar-focused wine bar on Taormina's main pedestrian corso; local food pairings with Etna and regional selections.
wine_bars.description: Casual wine bar with typical Sicilian food at the top of Corso Umberto; convenient starting point for Etna wine exploration with a well-curated island selection.
wine_bars.tip: Pair a glass of Etna Rosso with arancino before heading to the Greek theatre.

wine_bars.name: Enoteca EVOÈ
wine_bars.wine_program: Ortigia natural wine bar with a compact curated list of Sicilian and Italian artisan producers; retail and by-the-glass service.
wine_bars.description: Intimate Ortigia wine bar and shop specialising in natural and artisan Sicilian wines; lively after-dinner spot with a discerning crowd.
wine_bars.tip: Check their social media for natural wine producer pop-up evenings.

wine_bars.name: Bottega Monteleone
wine_bars.wine_program: Natural wine shop and bar in Palermo with local, seasonal organic food pairings; part of Raisin's verified natural wine network.
wine_bars.description: Palermo natural wine advocate with a tight selection of Sicilian and Italian low-intervention producers; small plates from local and seasonal suppliers.
wine_bars.tip: Ask for their current Etna naturale selection; producers rotate seasonally.

wine_bars.name: Inside Wine - Art Wine Bar
wine_bars.wine_program: Sommelier-led wine bar doubling as an art exhibition space; Sicilian and Italian selections with rotating artistic programme.
wine_bars.description: Created by lawyer-turned-sommelier-turned-artist Gabriella Maugeri; wine service is paired with live art exhibitions and performances in a refined Catania setting.
wine_bars.tip: Check Instagram for current exhibition openings; wine and art events are the highlight.

wine_bars.name: Osteria Ballarò Enoteca
wine_bars.wine_program: Market-wine bar hybrid inside 17th-century stables near the Vucciria; Sicilian enoteca room with street-food antipasti and natural wine focus.
wine_bars.description: Housed in the former stables of Palazzo Cattolica near Ballarò market; the enoteca room pairs Sicilian street snacks with natural wines in a theatrical medieval setting.
wine_bars.tip: Start in the enoteca room, then move to the restaurant space for full Sicilian antipasti service.

wine_bars.name: Enoteca Civita
wine_bars.wine_program: Natural wine bar and restaurant in Catania with minimum 30% natural wines; local, seasonal food and Sicilian artisan producers.
wine_bars.description: Catania natural wine bar within Raisin's verified network; seasonal Sicilian dishes and a 30%-plus natural wine selection create a relaxed, unpretentious atmosphere.
wine_bars.tip: Pairs well with a visit to the Catania fish market nearby.

wine_bars.name: Osteria dei Vespri Wine Bar
wine_bars.address: Piazza Croce dei Vespri 6, 90133 Palermo (PA), Sicily
wine_bars.wine_program: 650-label cellar curated by brothers Andrea and Alberto Rizzo; comprehensive Sicilian selection plus top Italian denominations; Michelin-recognised wine list.
wine_bars.description: Michelin-listed Palermo restaurant in Palazzo Gangi (the location of Visconti's 'The Leopard') with a 650-label cellar that is among the finest in Sicily; bar area allows wine-only visits.
wine_bars.tip: The bar seats offer the full wine list without a food commitment; recommended for solo wine explorers.

verified.address_quoted: Piazza Croce dei Vespri 6, Palermo, Italy

wine_bars.name: RIX Catania
wine_bars.wine_program: Bar, restaurant and wine shop with 30%-plus natural wines; local, seasonal and mostly organic food pairings; Raisin-verified natural wine venue.
wine_bars.description: Catania's lively natural-wine bar and shop with a creative kitchen; the 30%-natural wine floor makes this a gateway for low-intervention Sicilian discoveries.
wine_bars.tip: The orange wine selection from Sicilian producers is unusually deep for a bar.

wine_bars.name: Enoteca Robertone
wine_bars.wine_program: Natural wine shop and bar in Palermo with local, seasonal organic food; Raisin-verified venue with small-producer Sicilian focus.
wine_bars.description: Palermo natural wine bar and shop with an emphasis on Sicilian small producers and seasonal local food; community-oriented and low-key atmosphere.
wine_bars.tip: One of Palermo's most reliable stops for new Sicilian natural-wine discoveries.
